title: Energy resets to max each combat turn by default
summary: `PlayerCombatState.ResetEnergy()` sets current energy to `MaxEnergy`, while `GainEnergy()` and `LoseEnergy()` clamp changes so energy never drops below zero. Treat baseline energy as a refreshed per-turn resource unless another effect explicitly preserves or grants more.

title: Card play legality checks both energy and stars
summary: `PlayerCombatState.HasEnoughResourcesFor()` validates energy cost and star cost together. If a hook allows excess energy to be paid with stars, the deficit converts into extra star cost at 2 stars per missing energy before final legality is checked.

title: Block normally clears when combat ends and usually between turns
summary: The default assumption is that block is temporary. `Player.AfterCombatEnd()` explicitly removes all remaining block, and powers such as `BarricadePower` and `BlurPower` only exist because block would otherwise be cleared unless a preventer says not to.

title: Barricade prevents its owner from clearing block
summary: `BarricadePower.ShouldClearBlock()` returns false for its owner, so the owner keeps block across turns while the power remains active. It is a single-instance buff rather than a stacking duration counter.

title: Blur preserves block temporarily then counts down
summary: `BlurPower.ShouldClearBlock()` also prevents block clear for its owner, but unlike Barricade it is a counter buff. At the start of the player side turn, `BlurPower.AfterSideTurnStart()` decrements the duration, so Blur is temporary block retention rather than permanent persistence.

title: Strength adds flat damage to powered attacks
summary: `StrengthPower.ModifyDamageAdditive()` adds the current Strength amount to attacks made by the owner, but only when the value props identify the hit as a powered attack. Strength can be negative, so it can also reduce outgoing attack damage.

title: Dexterity adds flat block to powered block sources
summary: `DexterityPower.ModifyBlockAdditive()` adds the current amount to the owner's powered card or monster-move block gains. Like Strength, Dexterity allows negative values, so it can either raise or lower generated block.

title: Weak reduces powered attack damage multiplicatively
summary: `WeakPower.ModifyDamageMultiplicative()` applies a base multiplier of 0.75 to the owner's powered attacks. Relics and powers can further modify this multiplier, and `WeakPower.AfterTurnEnd()` ticks the duration down at enemy turn end.

title: Vulnerable increases powered attack damage taken multiplicatively
summary: `VulnerablePower.ModifyDamageMultiplicative()` applies a base multiplier of 1.5 when the owner is the attack target and the hit is a powered attack. Paper Phrog, Debilitate, Cruelty, and similar modifiers can alter that multiplier before final damage is resolved.

title: Frail reduces powered block gains to 75%
summary: `FrailPower.ModifyBlockMultiplicative()` returns 0.75 for the afflicted owner when the block source is a powered card or monster move. Frail is a counter debuff and its duration also ticks down at enemy turn end.

title: Poison deals unblockable damage at the poisoned side's turn start and then decays
summary: `PoisonPower.AfterSideTurnStart()` triggers when that side's turn starts. Each trigger deals unblockable, unpowered damage equal to the current Poison amount, then decrements Poison by 1 if the target survives. Extra triggers can be granted by Accelerant on living opponents, up to the current Poison amount.

title: Intangible caps incoming HP loss instead of just reducing attack text
summary: `IntangiblePower` overrides both HP-loss and damage-cap modification paths. While active, damage to the owner is capped at 1 by default, or 5 if the attacker has The Boot. This means Intangible applies as a hard ceiling to qualifying damage rather than a small percentage reduction.

title: No Draw blocks normal draws for the affected player's current turn only
summary: `NoDrawPower.ShouldDraw()` returns false for the owning player unless the draw is flagged as `fromHandDraw`. `NoDrawPower.AfterTurnEnd()` removes the power entirely, so it is a one-turn draw lock rather than a lasting counter.

title: Focus shifts orb values but never below zero
summary: `FocusPower.ModifyOrbValue()` adds the owner's Focus amount to orb values for that player, then clamps the result at a minimum of zero. Focus therefore boosts or suppresses orb effectiveness directly, but cannot push a final orb value negative.

title: Plating grants end-turn block and then loses layers over time
summary: `PlatingPower.BeforeTurnEndEarly()` grants block equal to current Plating when that side's turn ends. Afterwards, `AfterTurnEnd()` reduces the power: players lose 1 layer per enemy turn end, while enemies can lose multiple layers equal to the stored decrement value, which scales with player count when applied to enemies.

title: Regen heals at end of the owner's turn and then decreases
summary: `RegenPower.AfterTurnEnd()` checks for the owner's side turn end, heals the owner for the current Regen amount if alive, then decrements Regen by 1. Regen therefore represents a shrinking end-of-turn heal-over-time buff.

title: Ritual grants recurring Strength at end of turn
summary: `RitualPower.AfterTurnEnd()` applies Strength equal to Ritual amount at the end of the owner's side turn. Enemies that were just given Ritual skip the first immediate trigger once, preventing a same-turn bonus on application.

title: Combat cleanup removes temporary powers and remaining block
summary: When combat ends, `Player.AfterCombatEnd()` removes all powers except explicitly preserved ones, runs player combat-state cleanup, and strips all remaining block via `Creature.LoseBlockInternal(Creature.Block)`. Treat leftover combat-only defenses and statuses as ephemeral unless a system explicitly carries them out of combat.
